<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] CLAUDE.md and CODEX.md are listed as requiring synchronized updates across 14 sections; a lightweight CI check diffing the two files' shared sections would catch drift before it misleads contributors.
[observation] [info] Zero coupling is correct for a root zone: orchestration scripts spawn subprocesses rather than importing packages, keeping the tier boundary clean.
[observation] [info] architecture-policy.test.js and domain-isolation.test.js live in the e2e zone rather than here, which is correct — keeping policy enforcement in a dedicated test zone prevents root from accumulating test dependencies.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- High cohesion (1.0) reflects that all root files are tightly scoped to project-level concerns with no cross-package imports pulling them apart.
- The orchestration scripts (cli.js, web.js, ci.js, config.js) are the only tier-0 entry points and must remain spawn-only per CLAUDE.md; periodic audits should confirm no library imports creep in.
- Root config files (package.json, .npmrc, pnpm-workspace.yaml) form the monorepo contract — changes here affect all packages simultaneously and warrant extra review discipline.
- Zero coupling is correct for a root zone: orchestration scripts spawn subprocesses rather than importing packages, keeping the tier boundary clean.
- CLAUDE.md and CODEX.md are listed as requiring synchronized updates across 14 sections; a lightweight CI check diffing the two files' shared sections would catch drift before it misleads contributors.
- architecture-policy.test.js and domain-isolation.test.js live in the e2e zone rather than here, which is correct — keeping policy enforcement in a dedicated test zone prevents root from accumulating test dependencies.
- [call graph] 471 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
